steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content:
Find the comparison signal:
The passage sets up a clear comparison between the Wright Flyer (first, non-aerodynamic aircraft) and the modern Douglas DC-10 (far more aerodynamic). This indicates a shift from less to more efficient design. The lift-to-drag ratio provided for each plane highlights this contrast.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Determine what fits:
Choice C, “However,” captures the contrasting shift exactly between the two planes regarding their aerodynamic efficiency.

The other options either introduce unrelated conclusions (“In conclusion,”) or fail to effectively show contrast with weaker alternatives like “Specifically,” and “Nevertheless.”

This matches choice C — C is correct.
